Free Tips On attheraces.com Handicap
Spotlight: Back from a 91-day break to get off the mark in a five-runner C&D handicap last week; has a stiffer task with a penalty but still has possibilities.

RaceName: Free Tips On attheraces.com Handicap
Spotlight: Ex-Ruth Carr; won at Windsor on last week's stable debut (5f, good to firm); could go well despite the penalty but has yet to win in nine attempts on AW.

RaceName: Free Tips On attheraces.com Handicap
Spotlight: Ex-Irish; last three wins have been on Dundalk AW, most recently over 6f in January; has won over 5f, so should go well on stable debut.

RaceName: Free Tips On attheraces.com Handicap
Spotlight: Irish raider; neck second in a classified event over C&D on her last AW start; winless after 17 races but is one to consider.

RaceName: Free Tips On attheraces.com Handicap
Spotlight: Exposed 25-race maiden; creditable third in a classified stakes at Brighton in June (5.5f, good; cheekpieces tried) but not so good at Chepstow on Friday; blinkers now tried.

RaceName: Free Tips On attheraces.com Handicap
Spotlight: 0-11; runner-up in a 5f Newcastle classified in January when last seen out; not ruled out but may improve for the run.

RaceName: Free Tips On attheraces.com Handicap
Spotlight: Sole win in 24 races was a Brighton maiden in May 2024 (6f, good); fair run last time at Wetherby but others appeal more for the win.

RaceName: Free Tips On attheraces.com Handicap
Spotlight: 25-race maiden; not in much form this year and others look stronger.

RaceName: Free Tips On attheraces.com Handicap
Spotlight: Has won five races, two on the AW, but out of form recently and hard to fancy.

RaceName: Merit Estates Chartered Surveyors Restricted Maiden Fillies' Stakes (Bands B-D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Form dipped sharply on final AW start early last year for Sir Mark Prescott; seemed to need the outing when a 33-1 shot on stable/seasonal debut; the market signals may again provide guidance.

RaceName: Merit Estates Chartered Surveyors Restricted Maiden Fillies' Stakes (Bands B-D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Filly by Gleneagles; sibling to five winners, notably Hoodwinker (1m-1m2f; 92); dam unraced half-sister to 1m4f Group 2 winner Pongee; represents major yard and starts off in an uncompetitive race; interesting.

RaceName: Merit Estates Chartered Surveyors Restricted Maiden Fillies' Stakes (Bands B-D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Front-running, thrice-raced filly whose RPRs are moving in the right direction; good third at Beverley (8.5f, good) most recently; the pick on form and should have her own way at a track that is likely to suit.

RaceName: Merit Estates Chartered Surveyors Restricted Maiden Fillies' Stakes (Bands B-D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 30,000gns yearling; Mehmas half-sister to 7.5f 2yo winner Neardown (RPR 76); dam 7f winner (76), half-sister to 9.5f Listed winner Rainbow Crossing; stable isn't associated with debut scorers.

RaceName: Merit Estates Chartered Surveyors Restricted Maiden Fillies' Stakes (Bands B-D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Twice-raced filly who brings the worst form and should find handicaps more suitable shortly.

RaceName: SuperValu Co. Roscommon Ladies Day Novice Hurdle
Spotlight: Won a 2m3f Limerick maiden hurdle and unseated at the first in a Punchestown handicap next time; didn't jump that well and idled badly when just denied in a 3m Wexford novice last time; down massively in trip; highest-rated here but far from bomb-proof.

RaceName: SuperValu Co. Roscommon Ladies Day Novice Hurdle
Spotlight: Showed improved form to win a Ballinrobe maiden hurdle last time with the minimum of fuss; needs to find more again but is going the right way and has to be considered.

RaceName: SuperValu Co. Roscommon Ladies Day Novice Hurdle
Spotlight: Maiden over hurdles is rated only 74 so is out of his depth in this company; blinkers added.

RaceName: SuperValu Co. Roscommon Ladies Day Novice Hurdle
Spotlight: Just a hint of promise in five bumpers; however, it wasn't a bad hurdling debut at Listowel but this demands a lot more.

RaceName: SuperValu Co. Roscommon Ladies Day Novice Hurdle
Spotlight: Was poor in a bumper last year at Ballinrobe but was running well in a maiden hurdle at Clonmel on return when a bit unlucky to fall; this is a step up in class but he could give a good account.

RaceName: SuperValu Co. Roscommon Ladies Day Novice Hurdle
Spotlight: Down Royal debut bumper winner didn't build on that in Listed company next time; however, bounced back to win nicely on hurdles debut at Punchestown; this is tougher and she needs more but could find it so is considered.

RaceName: Free Bets On attheraces.com Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
OwnerName: B Keane, S Nugent & G4 Contracts
Spotlight: Has beaten just one rival in her two runs to date; needs to show improvement now switched to AW.

RaceName: Free Bets On attheraces.com Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Not disgraced on her debut at Nottingham (5f, good; 40-1) last month; may well show significant improvement now tackling the AW.

RaceName: Free Bets On attheraces.com Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Best of her two runs so far was on debut when beaten 6l at Nottingham (5f, good) last month; having her first go on AW and may be more of a nursery type.

RaceName: Free Bets On attheraces.com Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 95,000gns yearling; third foal; by Sioux Nation; half-sister to 7f 2yo winner Sovereign Ocean (including AW; RPR 97); dam French 1m winner, sister to useful 7f 2yo winner Muirin; worth a market check but may not come into her own until she steps up in trip.

RaceName: Free Bets On attheraces.com Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Found little in two runs at about 6f and now drops back to the minimum distance; tongue-tie worn last time is retained on this AW debut; one to keep an eye on.

RaceName: Free Bets On attheraces.com Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Bolted to post on her intended debut but had a better experience when third of ten at Windsor (5f, good to firm) last month; provided there are no pre-race antics she should run well on this AW debut.

RaceName: Free Bets On attheraces.com Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Unseated rider before intended debut; pulled hard in her two starts in June, finishing third at Windsor (6f, good to soft; 11-8) and not disgraced when mid-division in the Albany Stakes at Royal Ascot (6f, good to firm); now tongue tied on drop to 5f on AW debut; looks the one to beat.

RaceName: Napoleons Casino And Restaurant Leeds Handicap
OwnerName: S McMillan, F Reay & A D Brown
Spotlight: Posted duck eggs at York the last twice but this is a drop back in class and he's on last winning mark; however, still has a much better strike-rate on AW than on turf and his losing run is mounting up.

RaceName: Napoleons Casino And Restaurant Leeds Handicap
Spotlight: Front-running filly who ran creditably at Ayr last time when bidding for a hat-trick (maiden previously); remains in form and has a strong record (2112) under Lewis Chalkley; solid chance.

RaceName: Napoleons Casino And Restaurant Leeds Handicap
Spotlight: Prevailed narrowly in cheekpieces at Musselburgh (1m, good) last time, registering a first win since 2024; back up 3lb but remains well treated on peak form, which includes a Ripon success; headgear removed; should remain competitive.

RaceName: Napoleons Casino And Restaurant Leeds Handicap
Spotlight: Veteran whose 2026 wins at Ripon (1m2f) and Musselburgh (1m1f) show that he retains plenty of vitality; form dipped when back at this venue last time, so needs to rebound.

RaceName: Napoleons Casino And Restaurant Leeds Handicap
Spotlight: Dual AW winner for previous yard; far from solid on his form for current stable, clear best effort when 80-1 second at Wetherby on penultimate start; 0-7 in this sphere.

RaceName: Napoleons Casino And Restaurant Leeds Handicap
Spotlight: Respectable fifth at Beverley three days ago; largely solid record at Ripon features a success in 2024 and peak effort this season (close second in early June); likely player returned to this course.

RaceName: Napoleons Casino And Restaurant Leeds Handicap
Spotlight: Five-race maiden who has failed to progress in two handicaps this season; perhaps best watched unless the market suggests a bigger effort is likely.

RaceName: Napoleons Casino And Restaurant Leeds Handicap
Spotlight: Never dangerous over 7f in sole outing this term; has a record of 3-3 over C&D (all on good ground last term; 0-17 elsewhere) and looks very interesting returned to this scenario; favourably treated off 1lb below last winning mark.

RaceName: Napoleons Casino And Restaurant Leeds Handicap
Spotlight: In reasonably good form having made the frame in last three starts; however, still has a better strike-rate on Newcastle AW than on turf and this is a step back up in grade; 2lb out of weights.

RaceName: Sheehy Meares/Team Earley Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Low-rated Flat maiden is 0-9 over hurdles but has placed a few times, including in a Sligo claimer last time (2m1f, good); had a stiff task at the weights that day so it was a creditable effort after a break; needs to build on that but 0-27 overall strike rate is off-putting.

RaceName: Sheehy Meares/Team Earley Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Much improved to make a successful stable debut in a 2m handicap at Punchestown in May (2m, good to yielding); back over hurdles off a 10lb higher mark since two respectable runs over fences; player.

RaceName: Sheehy Meares/Team Earley Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Maiden's best handicap effort came at Punchestown in May on better ground, making much of the running and finishing fourth of 18; didn't build on that at Wexford later that month (2m1f, good to yielding) when a 17l eighth.

RaceName: Sheehy Meares/Team Earley Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Gained her sole win at Listowel last summer (2m, good) off 89; didn't threaten in her runs on softer ground this year but bounced back to her best back at Listowel last time when a close second (2m, yielding); she finished third off 2lb higher in this race last year; in the mix.

RaceName: Sheehy Meares/Team Earley Handicap Hurdle
OwnerName: Conor O'Connor & Colum Currie & Mark Devenny
Spotlight: Flat winner on heavy ground; 0-4 over hurdles but some decent efforts all the same; proved she handles better ground when not too far away in fourth at Downpatrick in a rated novice hurdle last time (2m2f, good); can't be dismissed.

RaceName: Sheehy Meares/Team Earley Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Won on the AW for John McConnell in April last year; no show in three runs over hurdles but may do better now sent handicapping; check market.

RaceName: Sheehy Meares/Team Earley Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Won a Limerick Flat maiden for Joe Murphy last year (1m, soft); didn't show much in opening hurdle runs for new yard, but there was promise in her distant third at Downpatrick on handicap hurdle debut (2m2f, good); shaped like this shorter trip may suit.

RaceName: Sheehy Meares/Team Earley Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Looked limited until a much better effort at Kilbeggan on his second handicap start when a 1.7l runner-up (2m, good to yielding); the first-time cheekpieces worn that day were retained at Downpatrick (2m2f, good) but didn't have the same effect; a bit hard to trust.

RaceName: Sheehy Meares/Team Earley Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: No show in three academy hurdles and was beaten when falling on handicap bow; made an inauspicious start to his life for new yard at Downpatrick last month when tailed off (2m2f, good); others preferred.

RaceName: Sheehy Meares/Team Earley Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Winless from nine but her best effort came in a maiden hurdle here last August when a 3l third (2m, good to yielding); quiet since, including when beaten 17l on handicap debut at Listowel (2m, yielding); needs more but maybe this track can bring it out of her.

RaceName: Sheehy Meares/Team Earley Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Best effort came in her third maiden at Tramore when 2.75l behind the quality Cousin Kate; two subsequent handicap runs have been way below that level; the blinkers are new.

RaceName: Sheehy Meares/Team Earley Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: No impact in three maidens and not much encouragement at Wexford on handicap debut (2m1f, good to yielding); tongue tie goes on now.

RaceName: Sheehy Meares/Team Earley Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Gained a first win at Wexford last summer off 4lb lower (2m1f, good); he may improve for this return but market check advised.

RaceName: Sheehy Meares/Team Earley Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Ended a terrible run of form when a surprise winner on the AW in November (1m4f, 50-1); decent efforts in two runs back there afterwards; his previous efforts over hurdles were dire but he has found some form on the Flat since then.

RaceName: Sheehy Meares/Team Earley Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Hefty odds in three maiden hurdle runs and ran like it; can only be watched on handicap bow.

RaceName: Sheehy Meares/Team Earley Handicap Hurdle
OwnerName: Profit In Your Pocket Racing Club
Spotlight: Veteran bagged a fourth win at Wexford last summer off this mark (2m1f, good); not disgraced in three runs this year, but more needed to win.

RaceName: Race & Rest - Wolverhampton Holiday Inn Handicap
Spotlight: 2-5 on AW, most recently at Lingfield (5f) in April off 5lb lower; beaten three times on turf since but can't be discounted back on AW despite going without any aids.

RaceName: Race & Rest - Wolverhampton Holiday Inn Handicap
Spotlight: Sole success came in June last year for George Boughey when 3lb lower; should be better for first run in nine months when seventh of nine at Newmarket (6f, good to firm) last month but may need to come down the weights.

RaceName: Race & Rest - Wolverhampton Holiday Inn Handicap
Spotlight: Has won here but at his best at Newcastle where he has gained his last four wins; can ruin chance by starting slowly but is not firing on all cylinders at present anyway; best to wait until he returns to Newcastle.

RaceName: Race & Rest - Wolverhampton Holiday Inn Handicap
Spotlight: Hasn't won for two years but made an encouraging return from his second wind op and a six-month layoff when runner-up at Carlisle (6f, soft) last month; holds claims if he can back up that run.

RaceName: Race & Rest - Wolverhampton Holiday Inn Handicap
Spotlight: All six wins have been at 6f on AW; consistent sort who ran his usual game race when third of eight at Southwell (6f) in April; has had wind surgery since and must be of interest if it sparks any improvement.

RaceName: Race & Rest - Wolverhampton Holiday Inn Handicap
Spotlight: Three of his four wins on AW have been over 5f at this track; has pulled too hard to give himself a chance in his two runs this year; needs revival on return to this track.

RaceName: Race & Rest - Wolverhampton Holiday Inn Handicap
OwnerName: Eric Broadwith And Mrs Ruth Carr
Spotlight: Three of her seven AW wins have been over C&D, the most recent in April when 5lb lower; has run well since but requires a strong pace to be fully effective.

RaceName: Race & Rest - Wolverhampton Holiday Inn Handicap
Spotlight: 1-17, her sole success coming in 2024 when trained by Darryll Holland; has been running well this year and looks sure to be thereabouts.

RaceName: Race & Rest - Wolverhampton Holiday Inn Handicap
Spotlight: Enjoyed a purple patch in 2024 when winning six times; landed his third win over C&D last August off this mark; handicapper has given him a chance after two defeats last month; should run well.

RaceName: Race & Rest - Wolverhampton Holiday Inn Handicap
Spotlight: Listed winner in Ireland as a 2yo but regressed sharply for Jamie Osborne; no sign of a revival on stable debut when ninth of 12 over C&D last month.

RaceName: Race & Rest - Wolverhampton Holiday Inn Handicap
Spotlight: Sole success came at Lingfield last September off 1lb higher; has ran well in three of his six runs this year, latest when third of five over 5f here last week (first-time cheekpieces, retained); not far away.

RaceName: Race & Rest - Wolverhampton Holiday Inn Handicap
OwnerName: Homecroft Wealth, Paul J Dixon & Partners
Spotlight: All three wins have been at this track, one over C&D; hasn't been firing on all cylinders this year though and needs the return to this track to bring about a revival.

RaceName: Mark's Choice, Ripon's Most-Winning Horse Handicap
Spotlight: Low-mileage 4yo who ran creditably (1l third) at Pontefract on reappearance and may well build on that effort; 2-2 at Ripon (maiden/novice events) during his first season last year and warrants respect back here.

RaceName: Mark's Choice, Ripon's Most-Winning Horse Handicap
Spotlight: Has mostly duck eggs in his form figures this term but won at Ripon (6f) in April and the return to this venue, combined with first-time visor, may have positive effect; scored over 5f last year.

RaceName: Mark's Choice, Ripon's Most-Winning Horse Handicap
Spotlight: Markedly more exposed than Goyard but is similar in one respect, being 2-2 at Ripon; recent form is respectable enough to put him in calculations off a workable mark back at this course.

RaceName: Mark's Choice, Ripon's Most-Winning Horse Handicap
Spotlight: Far from consistent this season but scored off just 1lb lower over C&D in May and has a fighting chance provided he's back in the same form.

RaceName: Mark's Choice, Ripon's Most-Winning Horse Handicap
OwnerName: Ingleby Bloodstock Ltd & The Ivy League
Spotlight: Prevailed in small field at Catterick (5f) last time; however, no certainty to follow up off revised mark in this deeper race against mostly rivals who already have winning form at Ripon; 0-3 here.

RaceName: Mark's Choice, Ripon's Most-Winning Horse Handicap
Spotlight: Has a record of 2-4 this term and scored over C&D (good) most recently; creeping back up the weights but remains well handicapped on historical data (peak mark was 15lb higher); major contender.

RaceName: Mark's Choice, Ripon's Most-Winning Horse Handicap
Spotlight: Veteran who is a ten-time winner at Ripon, most recently last September; led for long way in small field over 6f here most recently and appears to retain a sizeable chunk of ability.

RaceName: Mark's Choice, Ripon's Most-Winning Horse Handicap
Spotlight: Productive mare who has been successful three times this term and finished a creditable second in last two C&D attempts; came up short last time, however, and may be in the assessor's grip now.

RaceName: Mark's Choice, Ripon's Most-Winning Horse Handicap
OwnerName: Richard Fahey Ebor Racing Club Ltd
Spotlight: Failed to beat a rival last time, as well as failing to maintain signs of a revival; by no means certain to take advantage of a reduced mark.

RaceName: Roscommon Herald Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Record of 0-9 in point-to-points; best of four maiden hurdles was on debut at Leopardstown when a 23l sixth; moderate chase debut at Downpatrick in May when a 13l ninth; visor replaced the previously worn cheekpieces for his handicap debut but others are more appealing.

RaceName: Roscommon Herald Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Won a 2m1f maiden hurdle at Killarney in 2024; fairly lightly raced since then but her runs haven't been too encouraging, although ran well for a 66-1 shot at Wexford last time when fifth of 14 (2m4f, good to yielding); shaped like this trip was worth a go that day.

RaceName: Roscommon Herald Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Dual hurdles winner in 2024, the second of them coming over this C&D (soft); has improved with each of her three runs this year and was a 6.5l third of ten at Listowel last time (3m, good to yielding); didn't travel with much verve that day but her mark is falling.

RaceName: Roscommon Herald Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Won a Punchestown handicap hurdle for Paul Nolan in 2024 off 90; 0-9 chasing for that yard but made the frame on several occasions; much improved display compared to stable debut when keeping on for third of 14 at Downpatrick (2m6f, good) last time.

RaceName: Roscommon Herald Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Went closest to scoring when just denied in a Punchestown handicap over 2m in January (well-backed) on soft to heavy ground; raced keenly over 3m on return at the same track last month; 5-2 favourite at Clonmel last time but was a disappointing seventh; cheekpieces go on and Cobden rides for the first time so he must be respected.

RaceName: Roscommon Herald Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Improved efforts in final two maiden hurdles upon encountering good ground; rain-softened ground wasn't in his favour on handicap debut at Sligo (officially good) when fifth over an extended 2m4f; worth another chance on proper good ground.

RaceName: Roscommon Herald Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Improved for switch to handicap company over hurdles when third at Cork (3m, yielding) before scoring at Kilbeggan (3m1f, good) off 87; creditable return to chasing at Punchestown last time when sixth; winning rider back aboard and not dismissed.

RaceName: Roscommon Herald Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Placed in a point but failed to complete her two others; his 25l ninth at Tramore last time was his best run under rules but much more needed on handicap bow.

RaceName: Roscommon Herald Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Went close at Down Royal (3m, good) off this mark in October but failed to build on that in two runs afterwards on soft; more promising was her return at Kilbeggan (3m1f, good to yielding) when a 7.75l fifth of 14 back from a break; 6.25l behind Katie's Casper that day but entitled to improve over that seasonal return.

RaceName: Roscommon Herald Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Best effort by far came on her third handicap start at Kilbeggan last time when a 1.5l runner-up (3m1f, good to yielding); that was positive and she's a player if she can keep it going.

RaceName: Roscommon Herald Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Weakened into sixth of 18 at Down Royal on handicap debut in March (2m5f, yielding); poor over fences twice following that and pulled up at Punchestown back over hurdles last time; best watched.

RaceName: Roscommon Herald Handicap Hurdle
OwnerName: Something Simple Syndicate/J C Hurley Et Al
Spotlight: Pulled up in opening two handicaps and well beaten at Clonmel in May; however, he showed a lot more when fourth of 12 at Limerick last time; needs to continue on the up.

RaceName: Roscommon Herald Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Placed just once from 12 starts over hurdles; beaten 15.5l at Sligo over hurdles and tailed off at Kilbeggan over fences last time; others preferred.

RaceName: Roscommon Herald Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Won a Ballinrobe handicap hurdle in 2024 (2m7f, good) off 82; out of form in final two hurdle starts in 2024 and three runs over fences last autumn didn't bring any improvement; best watched back from a break.

RaceName: Roscommon Herald Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: 0-18 (14 over hurdles) but went closer than ever at Limerick over 2m6f (soft) last time when beaten just 1l in second under today's rider; up 2lb but she's of definite interest.

RaceName: Roscommon Herald Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Winless from 13 starts over hurdles but some good runs in defeat; bounced back from a poor Clonmel run at Kilbeggan (3m1f, good to yielding) when running on well to take third in the final strides; thereabouts off the same mark.

RaceName: Roy Grimwood Memorial Handicap
Spotlight: Eight-race maiden who has been below par in two runs this year; her mark continues to fall and first-time cheekpieces go on but hard to fancy.

RaceName: Roy Grimwood Memorial Handicap
Spotlight: 0-7 but has yet to run a bad race; placed for the fifth time when runner-up at Newmarket (1m, good) last month; deserves a change of luck and shouldn't be far away.

RaceName: Roy Grimwood Memorial Handicap
Spotlight: Off the mark when up in trip on handicap debut here (9.5f) in March off 4lb lower; below that form back on turf twice since; needs the return to AW to revive his fortunes and he could do with an extra furlong.

RaceName: Roy Grimwood Memorial Handicap
Spotlight: Consistent filly who has been placed on all five starts; ran her best race when third at Nottingham (8.5f, good to soft) in May; dons first-time blinkers and must be feared if they bring about any improvement.

RaceName: Roy Grimwood Memorial Handicap
Spotlight: Showed some promise in novice/maiden events but proved a disappointment when beaten 11l on handicap debut at Southwell (1m, AW) in January; first run since and suddenly has it all to prove.

RaceName: Roy Grimwood Memorial Handicap
Spotlight: 0-8; has run well in handicaps on her last three starts but she may find this distance a tad on the short side.

RaceName: Roy Grimwood Memorial Handicap
Spotlight: Off the mark on his return from six-month layoff at Kempton (1m, AW) in May off 4lb lower; below that form back on turf at Newbury since but may be worth another chance back on AW.

RaceName: Roy Grimwood Memorial Handicap
Spotlight: Won a maiden here (7f) in March; not disgraced when favourite in AW handicaps subsequently but below form on turf since; interesting if the return to AW can see her stage a revival.

RaceName: Roy Grimwood Memorial Handicap
Spotlight: 0-6 and not progressing; beaten 12l on turf last month; her mark continues to slide but she doesn't look ready to take advantage.

RaceName: Roy Grimwood Memorial Handicap
Spotlight: Recorded his second success, at Newcastle (1m, AW) in March off this mark, but has been beaten 43l, 58l and 27l since; first-time blinkers need to have a significant impact.

RaceName: Roy Grimwood Memorial Handicap
Spotlight: A triple AW winner this year, the latest over C&D in April off 4lb lower; has performed with credit in three outings since and should not be far away.

RaceName: Roy Grimwood Memorial Handicap
Spotlight: Off the mark over C&D last month and not beaten far in two similar contests here since; looked a non-stayer at Lingfield (1m2f) last time and must be of interest back here if ready to roll following a break.

RaceName: Roy Grimwood Memorial Handicap
Spotlight: Won his first two races here (7f) when trained by Clive Cox; yet to be placed in seven handicaps for current trainer; his mark continues to ease but hard to fancy.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Handicap
Spotlight: Four-race maiden; weakened into fourth in Yarmouth handicap (11.5f, soft) most recently, having made the running in first-time cheekpieces, but may improve returned to forecast faster ground.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Handicap
Spotlight: Record of 3-3 since being gelded includes 1m2f wins at Ripon/Beverley that represent solid form and suggest he'll be suited by 1m4f; transferred the ability to hurdles last time; respected with further improvement plausible.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Handicap
Spotlight: Eight-race maiden whose handicap form, latterly over 1m6f, isn't particularly convincing; may benefit from the drop back in trip but others have stronger claims.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Handicap
Spotlight: Eight-race maiden who is proving inconsistent but has possibilities on peak effort, namely short-head second off current mark at Haydock (11.5f) three starts ago; tongue-tie replaces cheekpieces.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Handicap
Spotlight: Ten-race maiden; rallied for respectable third upped to 1m2f at Beverley most recently, leaving the impression she'll benefit from tonight's even longer distance and return of headgear (namely first-time cheekpieces); shortlisted.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Handicap
Spotlight: Holds weak claims on his 7f/1m efforts but will be suited by tonight's new trip if taking after his parents; improvement looks possible on handicap debut.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Handicap
Spotlight: Only a modest fourth at Catterick (1m4f) on handicap/turf debut but that effort wasn't completely devoid of promise; soft ground may explain a lesser effort in visor since; now goes in blinkers.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Handicap
Spotlight: Seven-race maiden who is in a consistent vein of form, having finished fourth in last three starts (1m-1m6f); should give her running but still needs to prove she's capable of progress.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Mares (Ladies Pro/Am) INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Wasn't beaten out the gate in winter bumpers at Leopardstown and Navan and there were again positives to take from her fifth of ten back from a break at Punchestown last time (2m1f, good to yielding).

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Mares (Ladies Pro/Am) INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Form of debut effort at Cork last year (good) has received a couple of boosts but she didn't build on it when beaten 14.5l back there next time (good); capable of better and too soon to write off.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Mares (Ladies Pro/Am) INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Half-sister to winners Win Some Lose Some (useful 2m3f-2m7f hurdle) and Don'tstopbelievin (point); made the breakthrough at the sixth attempt in points in May for Derek O'Connor; hooded for rules bow; improvement needed

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Mares (Ladies Pro/Am) INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Dropped away on debut at Listowel at 150-1 last September; got closer at Punchestown the following month when fifth of ten; others preferred.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Mares (Ladies Pro/Am) INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Sister to bumper winner Kilbarry Leader; dam bumper/2m6f hurdle winner, half-sister to useful winners Tailors Hall (2m4f/2m7f hurdle) and The Westener Boy (bumper/3m chase); makes belated debut at age of seven.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Mares (Ladies Pro/Am) INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Half-sister to 2m4f-3m hurdle/chase winner Stringtoyourbow; dam point winner, half-sister to 2m5f chase/3m hurdle winner Winchester Red, out of prolific 2m hurdle/chase winning half-sister to Grand National winner Red Marauder; trainer 1-76 in bumpers last five seasons.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Mares (Ladies Pro/Am) INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Pulled up at 100-1 on debut last June but left that form well behind when debuting for top yard at Downpatrick a year later, a clear runner-up of eight (2m2f, good, 11-1); her RPR of 108 that day is the best on show in this field but must back it up.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Mares (Ladies Pro/Am) INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Jumped poorly when pulled up on debut at Tramore (2m, soft) and tailed off at Punchestown last month; hard to fancy.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Mares (Ladies Pro/Am) INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Dam runner-up in AW bumper, sister to bumper and 2m3f/2m5f hurdle winner Aggy With It, half-sister to 2m3f-2m7f hurdle winner Thanksforthehelp, out of maiden half-sister to high-class hurdlers Agrapart (up to 3m2f) and Afsoun (2m); watching brief advised.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Mares (Ladies Pro/Am) INH Flat Race
Spotlight: 10,000euros foal; half-sister to 2m7f hurdle/3m chase winner General Hubble; dam bumper winner, sister to useful bumper and 2m4f-3m1f hurdle/chase winner Aimigayle and useful 2m4f-3m2f hurdle/chase winner Emmaslegend; this will be just the fifth ride under rules for today's jockey.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Mares (Ladies Pro/Am) INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Built on a moderate hurdling debut at Wexford in August with a much improved display back there later that month when a 5.5l third (2m1f, good); went one place better in a Tipperary bumper when last seen in October (2m, good to yielding); in the mix on form but may improve for this return.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Mares (Ladies Pro/Am) INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Half-sister to six winners inc Gallant John Joe (useful 2m-2m6f hurdle), Space Cadet (useful bumper and 2m/3m4f chase), Jot'Em Down (bumper and 3m/3m2f chase) and Farrants Way (3m1f hurdle); dam bumper/2m3f hurdle winner; good lady rider booked; check market.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Mares (Ladies Pro/Am) INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Dam unraced half-sister to winners Mr Vango (Midlands Grand National), Ros Brin (2m-2m4f hurdle/chase) and Cheap As Chips (2m hurdle/chase); trainer 0-11 in bumpers last five seasons; best watched.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Mares (Ladies Pro/Am) INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Unsold for 10,000euros as 2yo; dam French 2m-2m5f hurdle/chase winner, half-sister to useful French 2m1f-2m3f hurdle/chase winner Dentor Des Obeaux; market can split trainers' two.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Mares (Ladies Pro/Am) INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Beaten 38l on debut at Cork (2m1f, good, 7-1); much more needed but SP that day suggests there could be.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Mares (Ladies Pro/Am) INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Yeats filly kept on to take third in the dying strides of a Cork academy hurdle (2m2f, good); perhaps unsuited by softer conditions back there next time when tailed off; big player here with top rider aboard.

RaceName: Call 01902 390016 To Name A Race Handicap
Spotlight: Yet to finish placed after ten starts (6f-1m2f); ran poorly on return from five months off over C&D last time and is some way down the pecking order.

RaceName: Call 01902 390016 To Name A Race Handicap
Spotlight: Goes well here; all four AW wins have been over C&D; third over C&D last month but below his best at Bath since; looks sure to go close at his favoured track.

RaceName: Call 01902 390016 To Name A Race Handicap
Spotlight: Got off the mark in a classified here (7f) last month; has more on his plate back in a handicap and up in trip.

RaceName: Call 01902 390016 To Name A Race Handicap
Spotlight: Has shown little in three AW novices; makes handicap debut and a market check is advised.

RaceName: Call 01902 390016 To Name A Race Handicap
Spotlight: 0-9 and hasn't progressed since joining current yard; mark continues to fall, though, so cannot be dismissed.

RaceName: Call 01902 390016 To Name A Race Handicap
Spotlight: Hasn't stood much racing in recent times and was beaten 20l when last at Dundalk when last seen; hard to fancy.

RaceName: Call 01902 390016 To Name A Race Handicap
Spotlight: Two of his four wins in 2025 came in handicaps here, one over C&D; has been below that form since and a resurgent effort last time came in a classified race.

RaceName: Call 01902 390016 To Name A Race Handicap
Spotlight: All three AW wins have been over C&D, the latest in a classified event in March; has been below that form since and the switch from a hood to first-time cheekpieces needs to have a positive impact.

RaceName: Call 01902 390016 To Name A Race Handicap
Spotlight: Placed three times on Tapeta for Iain Jardine at the start of the year, including over C&D; now 0-19, but ran respectably when fifth of 12 over C&D on stable debut last month; one of the likelier players.

RaceName: Call 01902 390016 To Name A Race Handicap
Spotlight: Landed a classified here (9.5f) in March but well held in three handicaps since May; sports first-time visor but her best chance of further success is in classifieds.

RaceName: Call 01902 390016 To Name A Race Handicap
Spotlight: 0-6 and did not fare too well on her turf/handicap debut when ninth of 14 at Windsor (11.5f, good to firm) in May; returned to her best when runner-up in a maiden here (9.5f) eight days ago and holds good claims kept to this venue.

RaceName: Call 01902 390016 To Name A Race Handicap
Spotlight: Got off the mark here (7f) in April off 6lb lower; not able to reproduce that form on turf at Windsor last month but she may have more to give back on this surface.

RaceName: Call 01902 390016 To Name A Race Handicap
Spotlight: Won a nursery for Jack Morland here (7f; off 3lb lower) last November; stamina inconclusive after finishing seventh of ten over C&D in March when making stable debut.

RaceName: David Chapman Memorial Handicap
Spotlight: Thrice-raced gelding who ran well in Ayr maiden (6f) most recently, without quite seeing out his race as well as the first two on the soft ground; may take another step forward now handicapping; shortlisted.

RaceName: David Chapman Memorial Handicap
Spotlight: AW winner on final 2yo start for previous yard; has achieved little in turf handicaps this term and is perhaps best watched unless the market suggests a resurgence is likely; drops to Class 6.

RaceName: David Chapman Memorial Handicap
Spotlight: Nine-race maiden; ran respectably in first-time blinkers at Wolverhampton (6f, AW) most recently; possibilities off a reduced mark dropped in class with the headgear retained; sole runner on the card for his Newmarket yard.

RaceName: David Chapman Memorial Handicap
OwnerName: Ursa Major & Mark Walford Racing Club
Spotlight: Six-race maiden last year for George Scott, going closest when 1l second in a seller; sold for 5,000gns after final start; market may prove best guide on stable/seasonal debut.

RaceName: David Chapman Memorial Handicap
Spotlight: Four-race maiden who is showing consistency, respectable fifth in Doncaster handicap (6f) most recently; may still do better at this level; not dismissed.

RaceName: David Chapman Memorial Handicap
OwnerName: D Jenkins J Glover R O'Donnell J Short
Spotlight: Ended his 2yo campaign with 40-1 success at Redcar (6f) on handicap debut; has failed to back up the form in two outings this season; one of only two previous winners in this field but isn't particularly solid.

RaceName: David Chapman Memorial Handicap
OwnerName: Ursa Major Racing Ltd And M Dods
Spotlight: Lacks consistency but it's best to forgive his latest effort (met major traffic issues); showed best handicap form when third over C&D in May; now 4lb lower and Rhys Elliott takes off a bonus 5lb; interesting back here.

RaceName: David Chapman Memorial Handicap
Spotlight: Thrice-raced filly who holds weak claims on form but may be capable of improvement now handicapping with Catterick reappearance under her belt; check the betting for further guidance.

RaceName: David Chapman Memorial Handicap
Spotlight: Nine-race maiden who is developing into a consistent sort, having made the frame in most starts this term; beaten only a head when favourite at Catterick (6f, good to firm) most recently; holds solid claims and his turn may be near.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App Handicap
Spotlight: Both wins came in May 2024 over about 1m2f when trained by Jane Chapple-Hyam; closest since when beaten a neck at Nottingham (1m2f, good) last month in first-time blinkers (retained since); still well handicapped on his best form.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App Handicap
Spotlight: Has four wins at this track, mostly over today's distance; 3lb higher than his last success in March but cannot be dismissed here.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App Handicap
Spotlight: Scored over C&D in December but all of his other wins have came on turf; exposed and others may have more in their lockers.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App Handicap
Spotlight: Recorded his first AW win when prevailing at Newcastle in March but held since and others are preferred.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App Handicap
Spotlight: Debut winner who hasn't offered a great deal in five handicaps; doesn't appeal.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App Handicap
Spotlight: Both AW wins have been at this track, one over today's distance; has shot up the weights since as a result of further success on turf; in good order at present, however, and shouldn't be far away.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App Handicap
Spotlight: Both wins have came on AW over 1m2f at Lingfield this year; beaten 20l at Kempton (1m) in May and it may be best to wait until he returns to his favoured track.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App Handicap
Spotlight: Has scored twice over C&D this year; was denied her hat-trick (8.5f) last month after being hampered at the start and denied a clear run when looking to challenge; may be worth another chance.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App Handicap
OwnerName: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid
Spotlight: Showed ability in novices/maiden in May; somewhat disappointing when last of five at Windsor (11.5f, good to firm) on handicap debut two weeks ago; drops back in trip on AW debut and not one to give up on.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App Handicap
Spotlight: Steady improvement in three novices at the end of last year; sports first-time cheekpieces on handicap debut and any market move should be noted.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App Handicap
Spotlight: Multiple winner at this venue and showed he can still be competitive when third over C&D last month but others preferred for win purposes.
